name: Rhode Island
agency: Rhode Island Secretary of State, Business Services Division

steps: Vote to dissolve according to your operating agreement or by majority vote of members
Wind up business affairs, settle debts, and distribute remaining assets
Notify all known creditors in writing
File final tax returns with the RI Division of Taxation
Obtain a Letter of Good Standing from the RI Division of Taxation ($50 fee)
File Articles of Dissolution (Form 404) with the Secretary of State ($50 fee)
Include the Letter of Good Standing with your dissolution filing
Cancel any business licenses, permits, and trade names
llc.notes: Rhode Island requires a Letter of Good Standing from the Division of Taxation before dissolution can be processed. The letter confirms all tax obligations are current. Allow several weeks for the Division of Taxation to process the request.

steps: Board of directors adopts a resolution recommending dissolution
Shareholders approve dissolution by majority vote
Notify all known creditors and claimants in writing
Wind up business affairs, settle debts, and distribute remaining assets
File final tax returns with the RI Division of Taxation
Obtain a Letter of Good Standing from the RI Division of Taxation ($50 fee)
File Articles of Dissolution (Form 404) with the Secretary of State ($50 fee)
Include the Letter of Good Standing with your dissolution filing
Cancel all business licenses and permits
corporation.notes: Corporations use the same Form 404 as LLCs. A Letter of Good Standing from the Division of Taxation is required. In-person filings submitted before 4 PM are processed the same day.

tax.notes: A Letter of Good Standing (LOGS) from the Division of Taxation is required before dissolution. Submit the LOGS request form with a $50 fee to: Letter of Good Standing, Compliance & Collections, Rhode Island Division of Taxation, One Capitol Hill, Providence, RI 02908. Processing takes several weeks depending on taxpayer history. Contact (401) 574-8941 or Tax.Collections@tax.ri.gov for LOGS-specific questions.

timeline.waitingPeriods: Letter of Good Standing from Division of Taxation can take several weeks to obtain

requirements.employeeNotification: Must comply with federal WARN Act for layoffs of 100+ employees. Provide final paychecks by next regular payday.
requirements.creditorNotification: Must notify all known creditors and claimants in writing with a deadline to submit claims
requirements.registeredAgentNotes: Registered agent must be maintained until dissolution is complete and all pending matters are resolved
requirements.windingUpPeriod: Entity continues to exist for purposes of winding up after dissolution per RI General Laws 7-16-47
requirements.boardOrMemberApproval: LLCs require member vote per operating agreement. Corporations require board resolution and shareholder majority approval.

includes: Every required form with direct download links
